title: Docker Backend: Reconcile and clean up only owned job resources
description:
## Problem

MoonMind has cleanup code, but it is not tied to durable container-job ownership.

`moonmind/workloads/docker_launcher.py` removes a deterministically named workload container on normal exit and includes `DockerContainerJanitor`, which identifies expired workload/helper containers from labels and TTL. `managed_session_controller.py` separately reaps sidecar containers and graph/socket volumes. These paths do not consult a canonical job record or Temporal terminal state, do not cover all job-scoped objects such as temporary networks/scratch volumes/credential directories, and can act based on daemon-local labels alone.

The canonical service requires cleanup to remove only resources owned by one job, preserve available evidence, refuse destructive reconciliation when durable ownership state is unavailable, and never remove shared images.

## Desired behavior

Temporal orchestrates terminal cleanup for each container job, recording an independent cleanup result. A recurring reconciler compares MoonMind ownership labels with durable job state and removes only confirmed job-scoped resources that are terminal, expired, or orphaned by a failed workflow/worker. It fails closed when ownership cannot be verified.

Shared daemon images, unrelated operator containers/networks/volumes, MoonMind service containers, and unowned resources are never removed by job cleanup. No path runs an indiscriminate `docker system prune` or image prune.

## Current evidence

- `moonmind/workloads/docker_launcher.py`
  - calls `docker rm -f` for the expected container name;
  - uses MoonMind labels and expiration labels;
  - implements a label/TTL janitor without durable `ContainerJob` lookup;
  - does not delete images, which is correct behavior to preserve.
- `tests/unit/workloads/test_docker_workload_launcher.py` covers normal container removal and janitor selection but not durable-state reconciliation or shared-daemon safety.
- `moonmind/workflows/temporal/runtime/managed_session_controller.py` has a separate orphan-cleanup path for sidecar containers and per-session volumes.
- `moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_catalog.py` lacks typed container-job cleanup/reconciliation Activities.
- `docker-compose.yaml` grants the trusted worker's Docker proxy container/network/volume operations, so cleanup must be narrowly label- and record-scoped.

## Scope

- Define immutable ownership labels/metadata for every job-scoped runtime object:
  - job ID and owner/correlation digest;
  - object kind;
  - backend ref;
  - creation/expiry time;
  - optional workflow/run/step correlation;
  - schema/version marker.
- Implement idempotent per-job cleanup Activities for:
  - owned container and writable layer;
  - job-created temporary network, when used;
  - job scratch volume/directory;
  - temporary registry credential directory/files;
  - temporary input/output staging files;
  - other explicitly registered job-owned runtime objects.
- Preserve durable logs/artifacts and approved workspace/cache data according to their own policies.
- Record cleanup result independently from primary workload outcome, including each object attempted, removed/already absent/skipped/failed, bounded diagnostics, and retryability.
- Add a recurring orphan reconciler that:
  - lists only MoonMind container-job-labeled objects;
  - loads the corresponding durable job record and, where needed, Temporal execution state;
  - removes objects only when ownership/version match and the job is terminal/expired or a documented orphan condition is satisfied;
  - refuses destructive action when the durable store/Temporal ownership check is unavailable or ambiguous;
  - supports dry-run and bounded per-pass limits;
  - uses leases/locks so multiple workers do not race the same object;
  - emits operator-visible results and pressure/backlog metrics.
- Handle recovery cases including worker crash after create/start, API projection lag, cancellation during cleanup, object already removed, label tampering/mismatch, and backend unavailability.
- Explicitly prohibit image deletion from job cleanup/reconciliation code and command builders.
- Integrate the reconciler with existing managed-runtime janitor scheduling patterns where appropriate without merging unrelated workspace/artifact retention policies.
- Leave sidecar-specific cleanup in place only until #3264 removes the sidecar; do not port sidecar graph semantics into container jobs.

## Out of scope

- Deployment-level shared-image eviction; covered separately.
- `docker system prune`, `docker image prune -a`, or deletion of unlabeled resources.
- Deleting workflow/session workspaces or durable artifacts under the job-runtime janitor.
- Cleaning resources owned by another deployment or backend reference.
- Preserving the sidecar as a compatibility mode.
- General host-disk cleanup outside explicit MoonMind-owned job objects.

## Security and authority boundaries

- The API owns durable job identity/owner records but does not directly delete Docker objects.
- Temporal owns normal terminal cleanup orchestration and retry semantics.
- The reconciler is trusted backend worker code with Docker authority and must cross-check labels against durable ownership before deletion.
- Workspace/artifact/image retention remain separate authorities; job cleanup cannot expand into those domains.
- Agents cannot set/override ownership labels, request arbitrary object deletion, invoke the janitor, or receive Docker endpoint authority.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every job-created runtime object carries immutable MoonMind job ownership labels/metadata.
- [ ] Normal success, failure, timeout, and cancellation attempt idempotent cleanup of all job-scoped objects.
- [ ] Cleanup outcome is durable and separate from primary workload/publication outcome.
- [ ] Repeated cleanup treats already-absent resources as success and never targets a replacement object with mismatched ownership.
- [ ] The recurring reconciler removes only labeled objects whose durable job ownership and terminal/expiry state are confirmed.
- [ ] The reconciler refuses destructive action when the job store/Temporal ownership source is unavailable, ambiguous, or inconsistent.
- [ ] Dry-run, per-pass object/byte/time limits, leases, and bounded diagnostics are supported.
- [ ] Active/nonterminal jobs and their objects are protected.
- [ ] Shared images, approved shared caches, durable artifacts, workspaces, unrelated Docker resources, and MoonMind service containers are never removed by job cleanup.
- [ ] No job cleanup path issues global prune or image-removal commands.
- [ ] Temporary registry credentials and staging files are included in cleanup/reconciliation without leaking their values.

## Verification

- Unit tests for label parsing/versioning, ownership matching, terminal/expiry decisions, dry-run, limits, leases, and fail-closed behavior.
- Cleanup Activity tests for success, failure, timeout, cancellation, already absent, mismatched label, and backend unavailable.
- Docker integration tests creating labeled and unlabeled containers/networks/volumes, proving only confirmed job-owned objects are removed.
- Durable-store outage/inconsistency test proving the reconciler performs no destructive action.
- Worker-crash recovery test at create/start/publication/cleanup boundaries followed by reconciler convergence.
- Security regression tests proving ownership labels cannot be overridden by job input and no image/prune command is issued.
- Temporal workflow-boundary tests proving cleanup retries are idempotent and primary outcome remains unchanged.
- Combined test with an active container and a terminal orphan proving active-container protection.

## Dependencies

- Parent tracker: #3251.
- Prerequisites: #3252 for durable ownership/cleanup contracts, #3253 for lifecycle orchestration, #3254 for owned-object operations, and #3258 for durable cleanup diagnostics.
- Uses #3257 for temporary credential cleanup and #3255 for scratch/artifact mount ownership.
- Blocks #3264's removal of sidecar-specific cleanup and the image-retention/end-to-end issues tracked in #3251.

## Documentation authority

- `docs/ManagedAgents/DockerBackendService.md` §2 decision 7, §9 auxiliary outcomes, §11.5 image lifetime, §13 (lifecycle, cleanup, reconciler, no global prune), §14 cleanup metadata, §19 rules 13–14, and §§21–22.
- `docs/Temporal/ManagedAndExternalAgentExecutionModel.md` sections assigning cancellation/cleanup orchestration to Temporal and requiring side effects in Activities.
- `docs/MoonMindArchitecture.md` sections defining trusted worker authority and API/durable-state ownership.
- `docs/ManagedAgents/DockerSidecarRuntime.md` as a tombstone; sidecar orphan logic is temporary removal scope, not the desired reconciler design.
